content.problemStatement:
Priya loves bitwise AND, but she hates programming. Help her solve this problem.

Given an array AAA of size NNN, let BijB_{ij}Bij​  denote the bitwise AND of A[i]A[i]A[i] and A[j]A[j]A[j]. You have to find the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), such that i<ji \lt ji<j and Bij=A[i]B_{ij} = A[i]Bij​=A[i].


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case consists of a single integer NNN, denoting the Size of Array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3...ANA_{1}, A_{2}, A_{3} ... A_{N}A1​,A2​,A3​...AN​.


Output Format

For each test case, output a single line, count of such pairs.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of pairs (i, j) such that i < j and (Ai & Aj) == Ai.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: all five elements are 1, every pair satisfies (1 & 1) = 1, giving C(5,2)=10 pairs. Second test case has only one element, so no pairs.
Only pair (1,2): 2&2=2 equals the first element.

solution.approach: For each test case we need to count ordered pairs (i, j) with i < j where the bitwise AND of Ai and Aj equals Ai. The condition (Ai & Aj) == Ai means that every bit set in Ai is also set in Aj, i.e., Ai is a bitwise subset of Aj. With N ≤ 1000 a simple double loop checking the condition for every pair is fast enough (O(N^2) per test case). We store the answer in a 64‑bit integer because the number of pairs can be up to N·(N‑1)/2 ≈ 5·10^5. The algorithm: read the array, initialise ans = 0, for each i from 0 to N‑1 iterate j from i+1 to N‑1 and if ((a[i] & a[j]) == a[i]) increment ans. Finally print ans.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, O(T·N^2) overall. With N ≤ 1000 this easily fits the time limit.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array (input), O(1) extra.
